jokes.text: And I hope you'll be seizing a more contemporary movie.
jokes.setup: Professor says 'seize the day' - a Dead Poets Society reference
jokes.punchline: Jeff counters with suggestion he update his movie references
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback showing Jeff's wit and cultural awareness, perfectly timed retort with good economy

jokes.text: Stroke or tai chi? Tai chi. Keep it up.
jokes.setup: Jeff sees Leonard doing slow movements
jokes.punchline: Casually asks if it's exercise or medical emergency
jokes.explanation: Clever observational humor about elderly exercise patterns, very economical delivery

jokes.text: It's called 'chillaxing'. Duh!
jokes.setup: Jeff asks what Garrett is doing
jokes.punchline: Garrett seriously explains 'chillaxing' like it's profound
jokes.explanation: Character-specific humor showing Garrett's oblivious nature, portmanteau word played straight

jokes.text: Oh, shoot. I forgot saying that summons him.
jokes.setup: Jeff says 'not much could ruin today'
jokes.punchline: Realizes he just triggered a sitcom trope by tempting fate
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-commentary on TV tropes, very Community-specific humor that breaks the fourth wall cleverly

jokes.text: Banana rhino. Banana Sam Elliott. LeVar 'Banana' Burton. Banana King Tut.
jokes.setup: Characters doing some kind of word game
jokes.punchline: Increasingly random celebrity/thing combinations with 'banana'
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate absurdist humor, loses points for randomness without deeper structure

jokes.text: B... banana penis. Sure, you would've loved it if he did it.
jokes.setup: Troy tries the banana game
jokes.punchline: Goes immediately sexual, then gets defensive about Abed's reaction
jokes.explanation: Relies on sexual humor and jealousy between Troy/Abed, not particularly clever

jokes.text: Remember how hard we laughed when we first heard the term 'teacher's aids'? Yeah. And then we found out a teacher did have aids.
jokes.setup: Troy nostalgically remembers laughing at 'teacher's aids'
jokes.punchline: Dark turn that they found out a teacher actually had AIDS
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation from innocent wordplay to serious reality, well-structured uncomfortable revelation

jokes.text: Money's tight, so I hope I can convince them for one more year that motels are tiny, little theme parks.
jokes.setup: Shirley mentions taking boys on a trip
jokes.punchline: Reveals she lies to her kids about what motels are
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parenting deception, perfectly fits Shirley's character and financial situation, highly quotable

jokes.text: Finally. Thank God. Hope he transfers to hell.
jokes.setup: Someone mentions Gary is transferring
jokes.punchline: Escalating hatred from relief to damnation
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation pattern, shows group's unified dislike for Gary

jokes.text: And there's a vote, and if you win, they put a crown on your head, and I am so jealous, Britta, I want to murder you.
jokes.setup: Annie explains transfer queen contest
jokes.punchline: Escalates from explanation to murderous jealousy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Annie character moment - competitive and hyperbolic, escalation works well

jokes.text: Amazing. He's like an evil genie.
jokes.setup: Dean appears again after Jeff's 'summoning' comment
jokes.punchline: Jeff realizes the Dean keeps appearing when things go wrong
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier meta-joke, clever characterization of the Dean

jokes.text: Some folks say 'transfer formal' isn't really rolling off the tongue, so we're just gonna call it 'The tranny dance'!
jokes.setup: Dean explains the dance name is awkward
jokes.punchline: Suggests even more inappropriate abbreviation
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean obliviousness to inappropriateness, character-specific cringe humor

jokes.text: You know, you don't actually have to lie on a sofa like it's a Woody Allen movie.
jokes.setup: Britta is lying on therapy couch
jokes.punchline: Duncan points out she's mimicking movie therapy cliches
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on therapy tropes, good cultural reference integrated naturally

jokes.text: Thank you for telling me that in the last session, dumbass.
jokes.setup: Duncan's advice about not lying down
jokes.punchline: Britta reveals this is their last session and calls him dumbass
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta snark, good timing revelation, economical insult delivery

jokes.text: There are bugs on the windshield of your mind you may never be able to squeegee, like a certain birthday party attended by a rather enterprising transient in a dinosaur costume.
jokes.setup: Duncan uses elaborate metaphor about mental issues
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to bizarre childhood trauma involving homeless person in dinosaur suit
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and absurd backstory detail, creative metaphor structure

jokes.text: So you think I'd feel better about myself if I got all sexed up, went over there, and really tried to be crowned queen of the dingbats?
jokes.setup: Duncan suggests the nomination could help her
jokes.punchline: Britta reduces it to 'queen of the dingbats'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta cynicism, 'queen of the dingbats' is a great dismissive phrase

jokes.text: Guess you really get what you pay for with free therapy.
jokes.setup: Duncan gives terrible advice
jokes.punchline: Points out the therapy is free, implying low quality
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and structure, perfect paradox of 'getting what you pay for' with free service

jokes.text: Your mouth looked like a coin purse.
jokes.setup: Duncan warns about lipstick
jokes.punchline: Specific visual comparison to coin purse
jokes.explanation: Vivid and specific visual comparison, economical and memorable

jokes.text: I have a counterproposal. How about I point out to you that we've never actually been friends, then laugh at your very well-deserved misfortune?
jokes.setup: Chang asks for help cheating as his 'friend'
jokes.punchline: Jeff formally proposes being cruel instead
jokes.explanation: Beautifully structured rejection, formal language makes cruelty funnier

jokes.text: No, Jorge, you killed it.
jokes.setup: Jeff laughs at Chang's misfortune
jokes.punchline: Chang tries to save face by claiming the laugh was too much
jokes.explanation: Chang's defensive reaction to humiliation, character-appropriate but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Before I respond to that, I'll have to take a extra-strength vitamin duh.
jokes.setup: Jeff says they can talk maturely about their breakup
jokes.punchline: Michelle creates fake medication for obvious statements
jokes.explanation: Creative twist on 'duh' response, good structure and economy

jokes.text: Why don't you take a picture? It'll last longer. Cool.
jokes.setup: Troy uses classic comeback line
jokes.punchline: Abed takes it literally and agrees it's a good idea
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment - literal interpretation of figure of speech, excellent economy

jokes.text: Then I think... 'What if we lived together?'
jokes.setup: Abed realizes he spends lots of time at Troy's
jokes.punchline: Drops heavy relationship implications on Troy
jokes.explanation: Awkward romantic subtext between friends, character-appropriate for Abed

jokes.text: He probably hates America.
jokes.setup: Someone asks why Abed hates them
jokes.punchline: Troy assumes it's because Abed hates America generally
jokes.explanation: Shows Troy's casual prejudice/assumptions, economical but relies on stereotype

jokes.text: I could be the next Yngwe Mackadangdang junior.
jokes.setup: Vaughn got recruited for hacky sack
jokes.punchline: References fake Swedish-sounding hacky sack legend
jokes.explanation: Absurd made-up name that sounds plausibly Scandinavian, good for quotability

jokes.text: It's not a Jane Austen novel. We have cell phones.
jokes.setup: Annie worries about not seeing each other
jokes.punchline: Britta points out modern technology makes separation less dramatic
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference and practical observation, fits Britta's pragmatic character

jokes.text: You were gonna move my hair? I don't know.
jokes.setup: Jeff reaches toward Britta
jokes.punchline: Awkward admission he was going to touch her hair for no reason
jokes.explanation: Captures awkward physical attraction moment, realistic uncomfortable interaction

jokes.text: See? It's alarming, right?
jokes.setup: Jeff comments on Britta being guarded
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates by making her flinch, then asks for validation
jokes.explanation: Shows Jeff's need for validation while proving his point about Britta

jokes.text: Just did my first keg stand! Ha ha! Can't feel my legs.
jokes.setup: Annie appears drunk and excited
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals dangerous side effect
jokes.explanation: Perfect Annie moment - innocent excitement about dangerous activity, good escalation

jokes.text: Dancing in your underwear, taking air conditioner repair so you can get a job, Greendale's the way it goes
jokes.setup: Pierce performs original school song
jokes.punchline: Bizarre lyrics combining underwear dancing with vocational training
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd school song, perfectly captures Pierce's disconnected creativity

jokes.text: He made that up.
jokes.setup: Dean calls Pierce's song 'venerable'
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercuts his own introduction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dean moment - tries to sound official then immediately deflates it

jokes.text: Finally, a classic last-day-of-school plot twist.
jokes.setup: Annie announces she's leaving with Vaughn
jokes.punchline: Abed identifies it as a TV trope
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed meta-commentary, breaks fourth wall expertly while staying in character

jokes.text: Hey, you don't have to tell us who Ingmar Mackadingdong junior is.
jokes.setup: Vaughn mentions the hacky sack legend again
jokes.punchline: Jeff mispronounces the already-absurd name
jokes.explanation: Callback with mispronunciation adds to absurdity, shows Jeff's dismissiveness

jokes.text: I spent three months researching backpacks before I chose the one that I use now. I don't want to be that person anymore.
jokes.setup: Annie explains her decision-making process
jokes.punchline: Reveals extreme over-analysis of simple purchase as character flaw
jokes.explanation: Perfect Annie characterization - over-analytical nature expressed through specific absurd example

jokes.text: But on behalf of the rest of the group, I hope it's a catastrophe.
jokes.setup: Jeff wishes Annie and Vaughn well
jokes.punchline: Immediately follows with opposite wish for the group
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff honesty, great structure contrasting personal vs group wishes

jokes.text: Is there a word in Spanish for someone who used to pretend to be a professor, but was a teacher, but wasn't actually a teacher, and he's now a student? Oh, if it was in Spanish, you wouldn't know, would you?
jokes.setup: Jeff asks for Spanish word for Chang's situation
jokes.punchline: Points out Chang wouldn't know Spanish anyway
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating burn, perfectly captures Chang's fraudulent teaching and Jeff's cruelty

jokes.text: I've put in a little... hot person. Hot people. There are two of them coming this way.
jokes.setup: Pierce mentions spiking punch
jokes.punchline: Switches to describing attractive women approaching
jokes.explanation: Shows Pierce's scattered attention and crude perspective on women

jokes.text: Cleanest face ever.
jokes.setup: Both women wipe Jeff's face
jokes.punchline: Troy's deadpan observation of the result
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy observation, great economy and understatement

jokes.text: You, me, and Jeff and rain man and big boobs and medium boobs and black boobs... We're a family.
jokes.setup: Pierce talks about the study group sticking together
jokes.punchline: Reduces everyone to crude physical characteristics
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce inappropriate categorization, character-accurate but relies on offensive stereotyping

jokes.text: And for the record, there was an episode of Happy Days where a guy literally jumped over a shark, and it was the best one.
jokes.setup: Discussion about 'jumping the shark'
jokes.punchline: Abed defends the original shark-jumping episode
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on TV criticism, perfectly Abed, defends the maligned episode intelligently

jokes.text: And psych! But they will get back together if you don't stop competing with her and start communicating with Jeff.
jokes.setup: Annie seems to reveal Jeff/Slater news
jokes.punchline: Reveals she was lying to make a point about Britta's behavior
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection serving character development, Annie being manipulative for good reasons

jokes.text: During paintball in the study room with colonel mustard.
jokes.setup: Britta asks where Jeff and Slater had sex
jokes.punchline: Annie adds Clue board game reference to the location
jokes.explanation: Clever board game reference mixing with show continuity, good callback structure

jokes.text: I've seen you shake the Dean's hand. Who knows where he's been?
jokes.setup: Britta is grossed out by couch sex
jokes.punchline: Annie points out worse hygiene concerns with the Dean
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and perspective on hygiene concerns, implies Dean's questionable activities

jokes.text: Holy macaroni with pepper Jack.
jokes.setup: Annie learning shocking information
jokes.punchline: Creative food-based exclamation
jokes.explanation: Annie's innocent cursing substitute. Creative food combination fits her character's wholesomeness.

jokes.text: Or may I say, 'meow-chelle'?
jokes.setup: Duncan greets Michelle
jokes.punchline: Makes terrible cat pun on her name
jokes.explanation: Terrible pun that fits Duncan's desperate flirtation attempts, weak wordplay

jokes.text: They're in the taco meat.
jokes.setup: Duncan asks who has her car keys
jokes.punchline: Bizarre response about keys being in food
jokes.explanation: Random absurdist response, economical but lacks clear comedic logic

jokes.text: Jeff and Britta did, uh, the Yankee Doodle. Oh, don't tell me that didn't make it to the States. It clearly originated here.
jokes.setup: Duncan mentions Jeff and Britta had sex
jokes.punchline: Uses patriotic song as euphemism, then defends American origin
jokes.explanation: Creative euphemism with patriotic song, Duncan's fake expertise continues running gag

jokes.text: You were the pit stop. He used you to numb the pain of not getting with me.
jokes.setup: Britta and Slater compete over Jeff
jokes.punchline: Britta reduces Slater to a brief distraction from the real prize
jokes.explanation: Sharp competitive insult with racing metaphor, captures Britta's aggressive confidence

jokes.text: He needs a girl who doesn't wear underwear because she hasn't done laundry in three weeks.
jokes.setup: Britta contrasts herself with Slater
jokes.punchline: Admits her own lifestyle choice is due to laziness, not sexiness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-aware character moment, perfectly captures Britta's messy authenticity vs manufactured sexiness

jokes.text: They should retire the table we did it on.
jokes.setup: Britta brags about her encounter with Jeff
jokes.punchline: Claims it was so good they should retire the furniture
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with sports metaphor (retiring jerseys), fits competitive moment

jokes.text: Okay, well, you still haven't. I'm just listing the nominees, so not a great time to get cocky.
jokes.setup: Britta celebrates winning before results
jokes.punchline: Dean deflates her premature celebration
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Britta's assumption, Dean being uncharacteristically direct

jokes.text: Jeff Einger, do not get back with Slater. I love you.
jokes.setup: Award ceremony is interrupted
jokes.punchline: Britta makes grand public declaration during formal event
jokes.explanation: Big romantic comedy moment, public declaration creates maximum awkwardness

jokes.text: Your lipstick looks better.
jokes.setup: Awkward moment after love declaration
jokes.punchline: Duncan offers random beauty advice
jokes.explanation: Callback to lipstick criticism, Duncan being inappropriate as usual

jokes.text: How can something that's delicious make me sick? Unless too much of a good thing... is actually a bad thing.
jokes.setup: Troy is sick from giant cookie
jokes.punchline: Slowly realizes basic concept about moderation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy moment - slowly arriving at obvious conclusion, character-appropriate naivety

jokes.text: I wonder where she got that from.
jokes.setup: Slater also declares love after Britta
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically wonders about the coincidence
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic observation about competitive declarations, economical delivery

jokes.text: Bring Conan back!
jokes.setup: Students shouting conflicting advice to Jeff
jokes.punchline: Someone shouts unrelated pop culture reference
jokes.explanation: Good random pop culture interjection during serious moment, captures crowd chaos

jokes.text: My name is Professor Ian Duncan, and I would like to rap for you. Drop a beat. My name is Ian Duncan, and I'm here to say, I'm going to rap to the beat in a rapping way, I've got a real big penis, and I drink lots of tea
jokes.setup: Duncan interrupts tense moment to rap
jokes.punchline: Terrible rap with crude and stereotypical British elements
jokes.explanation: Peak Duncan cringe behavior, perfect character moment but relies on crude humor

jokes.text: Who is it here who has a dalmatian fetish?
jokes.setup: Chang accuses Duncan of having problems
jokes.punchline: Duncan reveals Chang's specific bizarre fetish
jokes.explanation: Specific and bizarre character detail, good escalation in their conflict

jokes.text: My tailor will fit you for a uniform.
jokes.setup: Troy agrees to move in with Pierce
jokes.punchline: Pierce mentions uniforms for house guests
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce weirdness - treating roommate like household staff, hints at his controlling nature

jokes.text: And then we could all get together for some weird foursome.
jokes.setup: Jeff wishes he could live two lives with both women
jokes.punchline: Suggests absurd group sexual scenario
jokes.explanation: Sexual escalation of the parallel lives concept, fits Jeff's character but somewhat crude

jokes.text: I give this year a 'D' for 'delightful'.
jokes.setup: Chang reflects on the school year
jokes.punchline: Makes grading pun with positive spin on bad grade
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective wordplay, fits Chang's tendency to spin failures positively

jokes.text: I don't know any of these people.
jokes.setup: Pierce looks at yearbook after spending money on it
jokes.punchline: Realizes he doesn't recognize anyone in his own school yearbook
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce moment - disconnected from his own community college experience, good economy
